events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a commercial HVAC-R project at the "rough-complete" stage. The observed condition is "damper positions vs. balance report mismatch." This means the actual damper positions in the field do not match what the TAB (Testing, Adjusting, and Balancing) report specifies. This is a non-compliant condition because the system is not balanced according to the report. Since it's a mismatch, it's out of tolerance. The dampers are installed but not set correctly, so "installed-defective" and "non-compliant" apply.

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: medium (improper balancing affects system performance, air quality, energy efficiency)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"]
Percent complete: 85 (rough-complete)
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading given, but could represent the mismatch)
Confidence: 0.85
Findings: Damper positions do not match TAB report settings
Actions: Re-adjust dampers to match TAB report, re-verify balance
Rationale: Damper positions must match the TAB report for proper air distribution and system performance per SMACNA and TAB standards.
References: SMACNA, TAB reports
